name: Strategy / Observer / Factory (GoF)
def: Classic object-oriented design patterns: encapsulating an algorithm (Strategy), notifying dependents (Observer), and abstracting creation (Factory).
problem: Capture recurring OO design solutions for varying behaviour, change notification and object creation.
mechanism: Strategy injects interchangeable algorithm objects; Observer registers subscribers notified on change; Factory centralises and abstracts instantiation.
limits: Some patterns are language workarounds (now built-in)
failures: Patterns applied where a function/closure suffices
whenNot: When a first-class function or built-in feature is simpler
myths: "More design patterns = better design" — many GoF patterns are workarounds for missing language features
